body_txt: People respond to misery in different ways; some people might eventually move on from it, others might wallow in it for the rest of their lives, and still others might take their own lives because of it. One of the most harmful ways a person can express their misery is to share it with other people. Said misery can be something petty or major, but regardless of what the source is, some people will try to make it everyone else's problem by spreading it. Because there are so many ways to be miserable, there are many ways to share one's misery. Someone who is unlucky in love might seek to ruin relationships so others will share their loneliness. A person who failed to achieve their dreams might seek to destroy the hopes and dreams of others. A person who is an outcast might try to ruin the reputations of others. There are several reasons why someone would spread their misery around. Some might do it as a form of revenge against the source of their angst. Some might do it to draw attention to their plight in the faint hope that others might help solve the issue. Others may do so simply to make themselves feel better; in a twisted way, having others in as foul a mood as yours can help brighten your own, if only slightly. Super-Trope to If I Can't Have You… . Share the Sickness is one way this can be carried out. Sometimes this may be a result of Freudian Excuse . See Misery Poker for when people try to be competitive with their miseries. Someone who is Driven by Envy might do this. A form of Evil Is Petty . Compare Bad Mood as an Excuse , which tends to be a less intentional version of this trope. Also compare Taking You with Me and Hope Crusher . The Chain of Harm can sometimes be a form of this. Compare/Contrast Put Them All Out of My Misery for when a villain seeks to end their misery by killing others rather than tormenting them. Contrast Oppose What You Suffered for when someone tries to prevent others from suffering the same misery. Also contrast Sad Clown , for a sad or insecure character who copes through comedy and smiles, ironically spreading the joy they don't personally feel.
